'''Granicus''' was the name of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] throneworld, in the [[Granicus system]], a [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] with gray cirrus [[Methane/Legends|methane]] clouds. The galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] was a floating [[City/Legends|city]].

Prior to [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] served as one of 1,200 interpreter [[Droid/Legends|droids]] at the sub-embassies in the [[Granicus system]], assigned to a "third liaison officer" He served as an interpreter for 37 years before he was eventually redesignated a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] and sent to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outlands]] to help welcome new systems into the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]].

==Behind the scenes==
Granicus originally appeared only in the first draft of Star Wars, replacing [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] as the name for the Imperial capital. This planet's concept eventually found its way into the films as [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] and [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|Episode V]].

[[George Lucas]] revived the name when writing the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] [[Do Droids Dream of Metalloid Skies (Working title)|backstory]] for C-3PO in [[1977]], but the name never made its way into any licensed projects, making it as yet non-[[canon]]ical.
